rows.notes: Page title clearly centered at top, year legible as '1819'. Ink darker and unambiguous.

Description read as 'Civitat: Accom:' from left column. Amount aligned in pound column as '4' with 0 shillings and 0 pence; vertical ruling clear. Slight uncertainty in second word abbreviation but amount alignment is unambiguous.

Handwritten description looks like 'Mayor's Capons' (the second word is abbreviated). Amount reads as 14 shillings (no pounds) from shilling column; pounds column appears blank. Some ambiguity in description but column reading for 14s is consistent.

Description transcribed as 'Cler: Hastings' (handwriting ambiguous). Amount appears as '1' in pounds column and '8' in shillings column; pence blank. Column alignment modestly clear but description uncertain.

Description reads 'Cler. the Receiver'. Amount shows '10' in pounds column and a small '4' in pence column; shillings column appears 0. The pence digit is faint but placed clearly to the rightmost column.
Line 'Do. Subtreasurer' present but no amount visible on same ruled line (appears to be a sub-item). Description legible; absence of numbers clear.
Description 'Coll. Inn: Anim:' read clearly. Amount '4' pounds, '12' shillings, '6' pence align well with the three ruling columns; digits are dark and unambiguous.
Description 'Coll. Ball.' clear. Amount appears as 12s 6d with pounds column blank. Slight ambiguity whether a small mark at pounds is present, but alignment indicates 0 pounds.
Description reads 'Coll. D. Johan. Bapt.' Amount shows '8' in pounds and '2' in shillings, pence blank. Figures are relatively clear and within valid ranges.
Hand reads 'Supervisor Officiorum'. Amount '10' pounds, 0s, 0d clearly aligned and legible.
Single word 'Janitor' clear. Amount reads as '1' in pounds column and '14' in shillings; pence blank. Alignment unambiguous.
Description 'Bibliothecar: Dep: ad Fest. Nativ:' transcribed from the left. Amount '10' pounds and '10' shillings is clearly written and aligned.

rows.description: Do. Do. ad Fest. D. N. Bapt.
rows.notes: Second similar line 'Do. Do. ad Fest. D. N. Bapt.' Amount matches previous: 10 pounds 10 shillings; digits clear.
Line reads 'Concionatori. pro Decemb. 1818'. Amount '2' pounds '2' shillings legible and aligned.
'Do. ad Fest. Pasch. 1819' clearly written. Amount repeats 2 pounds 2 shillings; alignment clear.
Reads 'Vicar: Merton. Lady Day'. Amount '15' pounds clearly in pounds column; no shillings or pence.
Follow-up 'Do. Mich.' (same vicar for Michaelmas). Amount again 15 pounds; digits clear.
Text reads 'Vicar: J. Newington Xmas'. Amount '10' pounds evident in pounds column; clear.
Continuation 'Do. Lady Day'. Amount 10 pounds legible and aligned.
Line 'Do. Mid.' shows '20' in pounds column; handwriting of numeral is clear.
Second 'Do. Mich.' for same vicar; amount 20 pounds clear and aligned.
Word reads 'Pauperibus'. Amount appears as 1 pound 10 shillings; the '1' in pounds column is faint but consistent with alignment.
Description 'Postage' clear. Amount reads 2 pounds 10 shillings 9 pence; the pence '9' is somewhat small but in the pence column and valid.

rows.description: House & Window Assesd Taxes. Due Oct 10. 1818
rows.notes: Long description 'House & Window Assesd Taxes. Due Oct 10. 1818' legible. Amount 12 pounds 7 shillings 6 pence clearly written and aligns with total/subtotal rows.
Reads 'University Marshall'. Amount 2 pounds shows clearly in the pounds column.
Line appears as 'Corn Prices' (handwriting a little faint). Amount reads as 14 pounds 6 shillings; this is legible but somewhat unexpected as a large figure so confidence reduced slightly.
Description 'Watchman' readable. Amount appears as 12 pounds in pounds column; the handwriting of the '12' is faint and could be misread, so confidence moderate.

rows.description: Syndals & Procurations for L. Whittonham (undeciphered adj.)
rows.notes: Description 'Syndals & Procurations for L. Whittonham' transcribed; small parenthetical word after name is unclear. Amount reads as 2 pounds, 5 shillings, 4½ pence — the halfpenny symbol (small loop) is visible; pence digits slightly faded so moderate confidence.

rows.description: House & Window Assd Taxes. Due Apr. 1819
rows.notes: Second House & Window assessment line 'Due Apr. 1819'. Amount 13 pounds 3 shillings 6 pence clear and aligned.

rows.description: River Water into College from the City Works
rows.notes: Long description legible. Amount read as 5 pounds 5 shillings; digits clear and in correct columns.
Name appears as 'Lane Hemmings' (hand unclear whether 'Lane' or 'Jane' — chosen 'Lane'). Amount 2 pounds 2 shillings legible though description uncertain.
Line reads 'Subscription to the Infirmary. Xtn. 1818'. Amount 6 pounds 6 shillings clearly visible.
Transcribed as 'Grey Coat School - Michs. 1818'. Amount 20 pounds is bold and unambiguous.
Reads 'Kilington Charity School - Mich. 1819'. Amount 3 pounds 3 shillings clear.
Entry 'Yarnton - Do. Do. - Mich. 1819' (repetition abbreviation). Amount 1 pound 1 shilling legible.

rows.description: Insurance of College. Sun Fire Office. Mch. 1819
rows.notes: Long entry 'Insurance of College. Sun Fire Office. Mch. 1819'. Amount looks like 25 pounds 6 shillings; the '25' is slightly faint but consistent with nearby numbers.
Reads 'Syndals & Procurations. Kilington & Murton 2 fts.' — small abbreviation at end uncertain ('fts' guessed). Amount 2 pounds 2 shillings readable but faint.
Description 'Downs. Gardner' fairly clear. Amount reads as 7 pounds; numeral clear though ink a little faded.
Entry 'Land Tax for Coppin's House' legible. Amount 1 pound 7 shillings 3 pence matches the three columns and digits are readable though small.
Name 'J. Greener' followed by 'for lamp lighting' pretty clear. Amount 2 pounds 2 shillings readable.
Second 'Downs. Gardner' entry visible. Amount appears as 7 pounds 12 shillings; the '12' in shillings is a little cramped and faint, hence moderate confidence.

Another 'Syndals & Procurations' line mentioning 'L. Whittonham'. The trailing parenthetical word is uncertain ('for superscribed' guessed). Amount looks like 7 pounds 8 shillings 6 pence; digits smudged but decipherable.

rows.description: Eleven Poor Rates for St. Michael & Parish
rows.notes: Text 'Eleven Poor Rates for St Michael & Parish' transcribed; amount 7 pounds 5 shillings clear though the 'Eleven' is a little stylized.
Name appears as 'W. Pinduc' (last name uncertain—letters faded); 'for Bell-ringing' readable. Amount 5 pounds 5 shillings visible but handwriting of name reduces confidence.
Final total clearly written at lower right as '£270 7 1/2'. Pounds and shillings clear; the 1/2 pence (halfpenny) is distinctly shown as '1/2'.
